title: wibta if i refused to pet sit my friend's cat during the summer?
text: for the past 2 years, i've been friends with a girl (for the sake of the story let's call her l) who's an international student just as i am. we used to be super close, saw each other once or twice a week but since the beginning of the spring/summer semester, we've been quite distant, mostly because of the covid-19 outbreak. i've made efforts to talk to her or tried to arrange a call but she brushes it off saying she's busy with university work, which i respect. my biggest issue comes from the fact that she in return made 0 efforts to remain in contact. the only time she reaches out to me is when she needs help with university-related stuff and translating. when the restrictions were lifted a mutual friend of ours had a small get together to celebrate her birthday and she invited both of us, specifically because she knows that l doesn't have a lot of friends in this city and because l has been quite vocal about feeling alone (a thing i've confronted her about). i made an effort to remind l of the meetup and saying i'd love to see her but she kept saying she wasn't sure if she was going to come because of work. she kept making excuses and in the end told us last minute she wasn't coming. i was bummed out but what made me even more upset was the fact that the night before she shared in an instagram story that she went out to a bar with her boyfriend who she lives with.  here's where the cat comes to play. a month or two ago she asked me whether i will be willing to take in her pet cat when she returns to her home country for the summer holidays. i wasn't too thrilled but said i'll consider it since we weren't sure how the pandemic was going to turn out and whether we'll be able to travel back to our country + i wasn't sure if i would be able to have my summer internship, again due to the pandemic. fun fact: i'm allergic to cats. she insisted on getting me antihistamines which i wasn't too sure about either. in the end, it wasn't 100% agreed on but that's what worries me the most. she also suggested that she could leave the cat in her apartment (for approx. 3 months) and that i could come and check up on it every so often which i thought was a bit cruel and wasn't to keen on. i'm expecting her to message me soon and i'm afraid that if i refuse to take her cat in i'll cause her further mental health issues (diagnosed depression, anxiety). so here's the deal: wibta if i refused to take care of her cat while she's away on the basis that a) i'm allergic and not comfortable with popping antihistamines daily   b) i'll myself not be home a lot and have plans to visit my family for 3 weeks   c) i don't particularly feel like our friendship means a lot to her and have been feeling like she only contacts me when she needs something.   d) her cat might damage our apartment and that could get us in trouble? apologies for the long post.

storyboard.content: I've been friends with L for 2 years. We're both international students and used to be super close, seeing each other once or twice a week.
Since the pandemic started, L has made zero effort to stay in contact with me. The only time she reaches out is when she needs help with university work or translating.
I've made efforts to talk to her and arrange calls, but she brushes them off saying she's busy with university work.

When a mutual friend invited us both to a birthday get-together, L kept making excuses and told us last minute she wasn't coming. But the night before, she posted an Instagram story showing she went out to a bar with her boyfriend.
L asked me whether I'd be willing to take in her pet cat for approximately 3 months while she returns to her home country for the summer.
I'm allergic to cats. L insisted on getting me antihistamines, but I wasn't comfortable with taking them daily.
I said I'd consider taking the cat but never fully agreed. It was never 100% settled between us.
I don't feel like our friendship means a lot to her and I've been feeling like she only contacts me when she needs something.
I won't be home a lot myself and have plans to visit my family for 3 weeks, so I wouldn't be able to properly care for the cat anyway.
